county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1867 from Bracken, Harrison, Mason, Nicholas.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Olivet. Formed 1867 from Bracken, Harrison, Mason, and Nicholas. Northeastern Kentucky. Kentucky's least populous county. Very limited institutional resources. Robertson County Historical Society. Blue Licks area. FamilySearch microfilm. Check four parent counties for pre-1867 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1867 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1867. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Robertson County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1867.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1810 from Knox, Lincoln, Madison, Pulaski.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mount Vernon. Formed 1810 from Knox, Lincoln, Madison, and Pulaski. Southeastern Kentucky. Rockcastle County Historical Society. Great Saltpetre Cave records. Wilderness Road heritage. Livingston community records. Renfro Valley entertainment heritage (1939+). FamilySearch microfilm. Strong Unionist county in Civil War.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1810. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Rockcastle County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1810.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Morehead. Formed 1856 from Fleming and Morgan. Eastern Kentucky foothills. Morehead State University Camden-Carroll Library special collections hold regional materials. Rowan County Historical Society. Rowan County War (Martin-Tolliver-Logan feud, 1884-1887) court records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Fleming and Morgan counties for pre-1856 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1856. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Rowan County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1856.

Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jamestown. Formed 1825 from Adair, Cumberland, and Wayne. South-central Kentucky. Russell County Historical Society. Lake Cumberland area — Wolf Creek Dam displaced communities. Jamestown community records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1825 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1825. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Georgetown. Formed 1792 at statehood from Woodford. Inner Bluegrass region. Georgetown College (1829) archives. Scott County Historical Society. Georgetown is a Bluegrass horse farm center. Ward Hall (largest Greek Revival house in KY). Toyota manufacturing (1988+) changed demographics. Royal Spring heritage (first settlement). FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Scott County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1792.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Shelbyville. Formed 1792 at statehood from Jefferson. Central Kentucky Bluegrass. Shelby County Historical Society. Shelbyville is 'Saddlebred Capital of the World' — American Saddlebred horse farm records. Science Hill community records. Clear Creek heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Jefferson County for pre-1792 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1792 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1792. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Shelby County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1792.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Franklin. Formed 1819 from Allen, Logan, and Warren. South-central Kentucky on the Tennessee line. Simpson County Historical Society and Archives (excellent local collections). Franklin is a regional center. Cross-border families common with Robertson County, TN. L&N Railroad records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Taylorsville. Formed 1824 from Bullitt, Nelson, and Shelby. Central Kentucky. Spencer County Historical Society. Taylorsville Lake (1983) — some families displaced. Small agricultural community. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Bullitt, Nelson, and Shelby counties for pre-1824 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1824 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Campbellsville. Formed 1848 from Green. South-central Kentucky. Taylor County Historical Society. Campbellsville University (1906) archives. Green River Lake (1969) area. Campbellsville is a regional center. Tebbs Bend Civil War battlefield (1863) records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Green County for pre-1848 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1848 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Elkton. Formed 1819 from Christian and Logan. South-central Kentucky near Tennessee line. Todd County Historical Society. Elkton is on the old Louisville-Nashville road. Strong African American heritage — significant slaveholding county pre-1865. Robert Penn Warren birthplace (Guthrie). FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1819 to present. Continuous coverage.
